Pairing principle: Primary and paired recovery regions selected by latency, service availability, legal entity and risk assessment
Residency constraints: No cross-border replication for locality-restricted products without legal approval
Default recovery pattern: Tier 0/1 paired-region pattern; Tier 2 cold rebuild

Pairing principle: EU or country-approved region pair; use locality-restricted architecture where required
Residency constraints: GDPR, sector regulation, records and works-council constraints
Default recovery pattern: Prefer in-geography recovery; no global endpoint without residency validation

Pairing principle: Country/market approved pair with service availability and network reachability
Residency constraints: Data localization may restrict cross-country copies
Default recovery pattern: Local pair where available; otherwise risk-accepted cold recovery or alternative service design

Pairing principle: Map to approved regional plane or local deployment based on law and business criticality
Residency constraints: Regional service gaps and cross-border transfer approval
Default recovery pattern: Explicit exception and business-continuity workaround if paired services unavailable
